title: Simplify Group By UX with Toggle Behavior and Updated Labels
story: As a DataTools Pro user, I want a simpler way to enable and disable the Group By feature, so that I can quickly toggle between grouped and ungrouped views without confusion.
description:
Redesigns the Group By UX to be more intuitive:

1. Removes the "Group By" label
2. Updates dropdown option labels for clarity
3. Moves the dropdown to the same row as the icon
4. Adds a contextual tooltip when grouping is active: "Click to disable group by browsing"
5. Clicking the icon when grouping is active toggles it off (resets dropdown and removes groups)
6. Re-selecting the first dropdown option also disables grouping

The icon serves as a quick toggle — clicking it enables the dropdown, and clicking it again disables grouping entirely.
acceptance_criteria: Dropdown is positioned on the same row as the icon
Active tooltip reads 'Click to disable group by browsing'
Clicking the icon when active resets grouping and the dropdown
Re-selecting the first option also disables grouping
Gear icon does not overlap with the dropdown
ux_changes: Simplified Group By to a toggle icon with inline dropdown. Removed label, updated option text, added toggle-off behavior, and contextual tooltip.
implementation_notes: Initial implementation had a gear icon overlap with the dropdown. Ryan also noted that disabling grouping should reset the dropdown and remove groups from the table.
comment_summary: Balaji implemented. Ryan noted the gear icon overlap and that disabling grouping should reset the dropdown. Balaji fixed both issues.
